area: Midtown Park, North Hills, Raleigh NC
schema_note: Same local_xz coordinate system as buildings.json / roads.json (origin 35.8390, -78.6420). This park is the central green space referenced by nearby residents as 'midtown park downstairs' -- it's the social/visual anchor of the retail core, so it's broken out into zones + individual features rather than one big footprint box.
accuracy_note: Real, frequently-referenced park in the district (residents describe events happening there). Exact internal layout (path curves, feature positions) is a reasonable approximation for scene-building, not a surveyed park map.

park.name: Midtown Park
park.notes: Sits at the heart of the walkable retail core, ringed by shop blocks and overlooked by The Eastern residential tower. Used for resident events -- design it to read as a real gathering space (open lawn + seating + a feature element), not just decorative grass.

zones.name: Central Lawn
zones.notes: Core open event/gathering space. Keep this clear of props -- this is where 'events' happen, so it should read as flexible open space.

zones.name: Shaded Grove
zones.notes: Cluster of 6-10 low-poly trees with denser canopy, offset from the open lawn. Good spot for scattered bench props.

zones.name: Plaza Promenade
zones.notes: Hard-surface strip connecting the park to the surrounding shop fronts -- where outdoor dining/seating from nearby restaurants spills into.

zones.name: Play/Recreation Edge
zones.notes: Smaller secondary lawn strip, slightly separated from the main central lawn -- good for a couple of casual seating/picnic props.

paths.name: Main Diagonal Path
paths.notes: Primary cut-through path connecting the shaded grove corner to the plaza promenade, passing along the lawn's edge.

paths.name: Perimeter Loop Path
paths.notes: Closed loop ringing the whole park footprint -- corresponds to where Midtown Park Walk (in roads.json) runs along the park's outer edge. Keep this path's curve roughly matching that road's path for visual consistency.

paths.name: Lawn-to-Plaza Connector
paths.notes: Short connector from the central lawn directly into the plaza promenade hardscape.

features.name: Centerpiece Fountain/Feature
features.notes: Single decorative focal element at the park's center -- a small circular fountain or sculpture. This is the one 'hero prop' in the park; everything else should be secondary to it visually.

features.name: Lawn Seating Cluster A
features.notes: Group of 4 benches facing the central lawn, near the shaded grove edge.

features.name: Lawn Seating Cluster B
features.notes: Smaller bench group near the play/recreation edge zone.

features.name: Plaza String Lights
features.notes: Overhead string lights spanning the plaza promenade -- nice warm emissive detail for a low-poly evening render.

features.name: Perimeter Planters
features.notes: Small repeated planter boxes lining the perimeter loop path -- good InstancedMesh candidate, same approach as the building window grids.

features.name: Scattered Lawn Trees
features.notes: A handful of individual trees along the lawn's edges (distinct from the denser shaded_grove cluster) -- lower density, more spread out.
